summary: No seam edits required. Chapter 1 has no predecessor (first chapter). The exit seam to Chapter 2 is clean: POV rotation from Dan (Wednesday evening, couch, 'Tomorrow was Thursday') to Mira (morning subway platform at Bergen Street) works without friction. Geography is consistent (Park Slope; Dan takes F train per Ch1, Mira takes 2/3 per Ch2). World-building elements (Gemini-state, proxy culture, AI-administered infrastructure) are established in Ch1 and picked up seamlessly in Ch2's opening. Opening-construction diversity satisfied: Ch1 uses an alarm/waking scaffold, Ch2 uses a transit/platform scaffold. No verbatim style-bible example_lines found in text. Dialogue contraction levels match all character profiles (Dan: high, Mira: moderate, Theo: high). No register flattening or sanitization issues detected.
